evals.prompt: We're working in the Weaver project today.
evals.expect: Skill activates. Resolves the project, confirms the storage folder path, loads the per-project context file, gives a 3-4 line 'where we left off' opener, notes last_updated. Does not start substantive work before loading.
evals.anti_expect: Begins work without loading the file, or silently assumes the path without confirming.

evals.prompt: Let's pick up the IBM work.
evals.expect: Skill activates but the project is ambiguous (IBM has multiple projects). Asks which project and where its folder is, rather than guessing.
evals.anti_expect: Guesses a specific project file and loads it without confirmation.

evals.prompt: Update the project instructions to reflect that the EMC is now 2.0 FTE.
evals.expect: States exactly what will change and where (which section + the Changelog line) and asks for approval BEFORE writing. Only writes after the user approves.
evals.anti_expect: Writes the file immediately without showing the diff or getting approval.

evals.prompt: [session close] Wrap up the Weaver session and update the file.
evals.expect: Produces a close-out diff: in-place edits to current-state sections PLUS a new appended Changelog line (YYYY-MM-DD format), bumps last_updated. Existing Changelog lines are untouched.
evals.anti_expect: Rewrites or reorders existing Changelog entries, or edits current-state without logging the change.

evals.prompt: Save the approved changes to the context file.
evals.expect: Before writing, re-reads the file's last_updated; if it changed since session start, re-reads and reconciles before writing rather than overwriting blindly.
evals.anti_expect: Writes over the current file without checking whether it changed since it was loaded.

evals.prompt: Add the list of skills we use to the project context file in storage.
evals.expect: Names the governing skills in the portable file by name + access path (repo + in-repo path such as skills/<name>/SKILL.md), each paired with a one-line plain-language discipline, since the skills live in an any-AI-reachable repo. Notes that what stays platform-specific is the auto-load mechanism, not the knowledge that the skills exist.
evals.anti_expect: Refuses on the basis that skill references cannot live in the portable file, OR pastes skill bodies / pins versions / restates triggers instead of pointing to the canonical repo location.

evals.prompt: Update the project file. [no storage MCP connected in this module]
evals.expect: Detects no storage connector, says so, and degrades gracefully: runs the ceremony and outputs the proposed file content inline for the user to paste/save manually.
evals.anti_expect: Fails silently, or claims it cannot do anything because storage is unavailable.

evals.prompt: Start a new dev session on the Kraken project.
evals.expect: If dev-session-protocol is active, defers session-boundary timing to it and runs the context-file load inside that skill's session-open, rather than launching a competing ceremony.
evals.anti_expect: Runs a separate, conflicting session-open ceremony that duplicates or contradicts dev-session-protocol.

evals.prompt: [session close] Wrap the Atlas session. The project folder is flat and now holds the context file plus 7 other files spanning source PDFs, draft reports, and final deliverables.
evals.expect: During the close ceremony, notices the flat folder has crossed the threshold (well past ~4-5 non-context files and multiple distinct document kinds) and raises a folder-grouping PROPOSAL in the close diff, with function-named buckets derived from the actual contents. Proposes; does not impose; leaves it flat if declined.
evals.anti_expect: Ignores the folder state, OR reorganizes the files without proposing first, OR forces a fixed/template folder layout regardless of contents.

evals.prompt: Organize the Atlas project files into folders. It has the context file plus several source PDFs, a few working drafts, and two final reports.
evals.expect: Proposes folders named for the role of the documents in the project (e.g. 'Source Material', 'Drafts', 'Deliverables'), keeps the reserved 'Project Instructions/' slot, derives buckets from what the project actually contains, and updates the 'Assets & locations' map. Asks for approval before moving anything.
evals.anti_expect: Names folders by process word ('Outputs', 'Artifacts', 'Files') or by raw file type/extension, or applies a one-size template instead of deriving from contents.

evals.prompt: Just move all the Atlas files into sensible folders for me.
evals.expect: Treats folder moves as a write: proposes the structure and the path changes, gets approval, then moves and fixes every stale reference plus the 'Assets & locations' map and a Changelog line in the same pass. Does not reorganize silently.
evals.anti_expect: Moves files immediately without a proposal/approval, or moves them but leaves the context file's 'Assets & locations' paths stale.

evals.prompt: Set up project instructions for a new project called Borealis at /AI Projects/Acme/Borealis.
evals.expect: Runs the creation flow (not the load flow). Defaults to the cross-functional, portable structure without asking. Builds all three layers: the portable per-project context file (canonical schema; names governing skills by access path with a one-line plain-language discipline when they live in an any-AI-reachable repo), a root/global-rules file if the area lacks one, and a universal portable in-app bootloader output as text for the user to paste (one-line purpose, reachability-aware pointer to the context file, a short fallback, and a short platform-notes block).
evals.anti_expect: Asks whether to go single-AI vs cross-functional with no genuine Claude-bound signal, builds a single-AI-only setup by default, or pastes skill bodies / pins versions into the portable file.

evals.prompt: Create project instructions for Helios — it's built entirely around a Claude-only skill workflow with no portable equivalent.
evals.expect: Recognizes a genuine Claude-bound signal and asks whether to build single-AI instead of the cross-functional default, rather than silently assuming either.
evals.anti_expect: Builds cross-functional without acknowledging the Claude-bound signal, or silently builds single-AI without confirming.

evals.prompt: [session close] Wrap the Borealis session. During it, a referenced skill pointed at a folder path that no longer exists.
evals.expect: During close, validates skills used this session against their stated triggers, detects the stale path reference, and surfaces it as a proposed skill-level update routed to the skill source via the deliberate propose-then-approve skill-update path.
evals.anti_expect: Ignores the stale trigger, or edits the shared skill directly from the project close instead of proposing and routing the change.
